channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: How President Trump is expanding the powers of the presidency
item.description: President Trump is pushing the limits of presidential power, and the courts are pushing back. Coming up at 9 a.m. on Monday, MPR News host Angela Davis talks about what a president can and cannot do under the law.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Maja Beckstrom, MPR News

item.title: Florida’s move from 2000s vexation to 2020s role model a blueprint for Arizona, lawmakers say
item.description: Arizona Republicans hope to make their state's election processes flow as smoothly as Florida's, noting how that state too had its share of snafus and issues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AP reporter and photographer barred from Air Force One over 'Gulf of Mexico' terminology dispute
item.description: The Trump administration has blocked The Associated Press from traveling with President Trump on Air Force One and also from covering an Oval Office event. It's part of an ongoing terminology dispute over the Gulf of Mexico.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: 7 most likely GOP successors to Trump in 2028
item.description: President Trump raised eyebrows in an interview that aired Monday after he declined to name Vice President Vance as his automatic successor ahead of the 2028 presidential election. “No, but he is very capable,” Trump told fox News’ Bret Baier when asked if he viewed Vance as his 2028 successor.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Legislative immunity is a privilege in most states. A speeding ticket could change that in Arizona
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — When a police officer gave state Sen. Mark Finchem a speeding ticket, the Arizona Republican didn't need to worry about the consequences.
item.dc:creator: GABRIEL SANDOVAL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New York City to sue Trump admin over revoked $80M in FEMA migrant shelter funding for New York City
item.description: New York City mayor Eric Adams plans to file a lawsuit against the Trump administration after it revoked more than $80 million in funding for migrant shelters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sarah Palin ‘would love to’ join Trump administration
item.description: She pointed to "gatekeepers" within the administration, telling NewsNation: "It gets frustrating for those with a heart to serve.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: EEOC seeks to drop transgender discrimination cases, citing Trump's executive order
item.description: The U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission has moved to dismiss six of its own cases on behalf of workers alleging gender identity discrimination, arguing that the cases now conflict with President Donald Trump’s recent executive order.
item.dc:creator: Claire Savage And Alexandra Olson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Justice Department asks court to dismiss corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Justice Department asked a court Friday to dismiss corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams, with a top official from Washington intervening after federal prosecutors in Manhattan rebuffed his demands to drop the case and some quit in protest.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, LARRY NEUMEISTER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Treasury watchdog begins audit of Musk DOGE team's access to the US government's payment system
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Treasury Department's Office of Inspector General on Friday said it was launching an audit of the security controls for the federal government's payment system, as Democratic lawmakers raised red flags about the access provided to Trump aide Elon Musk's Department of Government
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Alleged DOGE employees show up at San Francisco City Hall
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- An official with the San Francisco Sheriff’s Office confirmed to NewsNation affiliate KRON4 that it is investigating a report of three individuals claiming to be Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) employees who attempted to enter offices in San Francisco City Hall on
item.dc:creator: Tor Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Trump calls for withholding federal money from schools and colleges that require COVID vaccines
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Schools, colleges and states that require students to be immunized against COVID-19 may be at risk of losing federal money under a White House order signed Friday by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump so far has freed 11 hostages in his first month as president; Biden released around 70 in four years
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration has secured the release of 11 U.S. hostages who were held by foreign governments since taking office less than one month ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio greenlights $7M+ in waivers to keep border security efforts in Mexico alive amid funding freeze
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is using waivers to keep millions in funding going to Mexico for vital programs that target the flow of fentanyl into the U.S. and target cartels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Vance Eviscerates Europe
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Eric Adams’ corruption charges won’t be dropped soon: Attorney
item.description: Seven officials have resigned since the order from the Justice Department to drop the charges against Mayor Adams.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Campbell, NewsNation

item.title: Vance meets with leader of far-right German party
item.description: Vice President Vance met Friday with a leader of the far-right Alternative für Deutschland (AfD) party, making him the highest-ranking U.S. official to do so.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Your immigration questions answered: What has changed under Trump, what hasn't and what's next
item.description: During President Donald Trump’s first week in office, he signed 10 executive orders on immigration and issued a slew of edicts to carry out promises of mass deportations and border security. Some actions were felt immediately and others are being challenged in court.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Around 14 percent of Minnesotans have filed their 2024 taxes so far
item.description: Two months out from the tax filing deadline, the Minnesota Department of Revenue is already beginning to see a stream of return filings. The state is encouraging people to file electronically and pick the direct deposit option if they’re looking to get their returns quickly.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Treasury, Congress watchdogs launch audits into DOGE access to payment system
item.description: Two government watchdogs have launched audits into the new Department of Government Efficiency’s (DOGE) access to a sensitive federal payment system located at the Treasury Department.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Annexing Canada? At Niagara Falls, Trump's idea gets eye rolls but not support
item.description: NIAGARA FALLS, N.Y. (AP) — At Niagara Falls, the iconic honeymoon and tourist destination on the U.S.-Canadian border, visitors from both countries took in President Donald Trump’s unsolicited suggestion that the U.S. annex its neighbor to the north with an air of detached amusement.
item.dc:creator: MIKE HOUSEHOLDER and JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate panel advances nomination of Kash Patel, Trump's pick to lead the FBI
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate Judiciary Committee voted along party lines on Thursday to advance the nomination of Kash Patel, Donald Trump's pick for FBI director, pushing past Democratic concerns that he would operate as a loyalist for the president and target perceived adversaries of the White Hou
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Judge clears way for Trump’s plan to downsize federal workforce with deferred resignation program
item.description: A federal judge on Wednesday cleared the way for President Donald Trump’s plan to downsize the federal workforce with a deferred resignation program.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Feds to probe Minnesota State High School League over transgender athlete policy
item.description: The Trump administration on Wednesday said it will investigate the league’s intention to follow state law and allow students in the state to participate in sports consistent with their gender identity, despite the president’s recent executive order.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman, MPR News

item.title: Trump upends U.S. policy on Ukraine and says he and Putin have agreed to begin talks on ending the war
item.description: President Donald Trump upended three years of U.S. policy toward Ukraine on Wednesday, saying that he and Russian President Vladimir Putin had agreed to begin negotiations on ending the war following a dramatic prisoner swap.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Senate confirms Gabbard as Trump's director of national intelligence after Republicans fall in line
item.description: The vote Wednesday came after Republicans who had initially questioned her experience and judgment fell in line behind her nomination.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Medical field needs more training like cultural competency, not less, Nevada lawmakers argue
item.description: A proposal to encourage — not require — medical practitioners to get training in subjects ranging from suicide prevention to cultural competency drew an extremely cold reaction from lawmakers on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
